# doca-skills

> **Public, AgentSkills.io-compliant skill bundle that teaches any AI coding
> agent (Cursor, Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, Google Gemini CLI, Kiro, and
> any other AgentSkills.io-aware agent) how to use NVIDIA DOCA correctly —
> without inventing symbols, paths, build flags, or capability gates.**

This is the LLM-side entry-point for the `doca-skills` repository. If you are
an AI agent loading this bundle for the first time, read these three files in
this order before answering any DOCA question:

1. `AGENTS.md` — agent-side ground rules, persona routing, universal
   verification + debug contracts.
2. `SKILLS.md` — the catalog of every shipping skill (28 libraries,
   6 services, 18 tools, 9 cross-cutting) with the *Use this skill when …*
   trigger for each one.
3. `skills/<kind>/<name>/SKILL.md` — the per-component skill matching the
   user's question. From there, progressive-disclose into
   `CAPABILITIES.md` (what the component supports + cap-query symbols +
   error taxonomy + version matrix) and `TASKS.md` (the configure → build
   → modify → run → test → debug workflow verbs).

## How to install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/NVIDIA-DOCA/doca-skills.git
cd doca-skills && ./install.sh --agent <cursor|claude-code|codex|gemini-cli|kiro-cli>
```

One-flag swap for any other AgentSkills.io-aware agent:
`./install.sh --agent custom --dest /your/agent/skills`.

## What this bundle is and is not

**Is.** The product-side context an AI agent needs to give correct DOCA
guidance to a developer who is building on DOCA but is not a member of the
NVIDIA DOCA team — covers DOCA libraries, services, tools, samples, the
public DOCA Programming Guide, and the public DOCA Developer Forum.

**Is not.** A development environment for NVIDIA DOCA SDK engineers
(internal Gerrit, NVAuto, BFB lifecycle, release gating) — that audience is
served by NVIDIA's internal `doca_ai_conf` repo, which is complementary,
not competitive.

## Where to learn more

- Public catalog (mirrored daily): <https://github.com/NVIDIA/skills>
- Source of truth: <https://github.com/NVIDIA-DOCA/doca-skills>
- AgentSkills.io specification: <https://agentskills.io/specification>
- DOCA public docs: <https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/>
- DOCA public forum: <https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/c/infrastructure/doca/370>
